source: FAA AIM Chapter 3, 14 CFR Part 91, AOPA

classes.location: Entire US domestic airspace at and above 18,000' MSL
classes.entry_requirement: ATC clearance with IFR flight plan
classes.speed_limit: No class-specific speed limit (aircraft Vmo/Mmo and ATC assignments still apply)

chart_depiction.description: Not depicted on sectional charts (Class A is defined by altitude, not geographic boundary)

classes.notes: No VFR operations permitted. Covers en route high-altitude environment.

classes.altitude: Surface to individually specified ceiling (typically ~10,000' MSL)
classes.location: Surrounds the busiest US airports (e.g., LAX, ORD, ATL)
classes.ads_b: Required (1090ES or UAT) within Class B, above the ceiling within lateral boundaries up to 10,000' MSL, and within 30 NM Mode C veil (surface to 10,000' MSL)
classes.speed_limit: 250 KIAS below 10,000' MSL (14 CFR 91.117(b) 200 KIAS near-airport rule does NOT apply inside Class B; 14 CFR 91.117(c) 200 KIAS applies to airspace underlying Class B and VFR corridors through Class B)

chart_depiction.description: Solid blue lines with MSL altitude labels for each segment

classes.notes: Student pilots require specific ground/flight training and logbook endorsement for each specific Class B area — each area requires its own endorsement (14 CFR 61.95).

classes.location: Surrounds mid-size towered airports with radar approach control
classes.entry_requirement: Two-way radio contact established with ATC (not clearance — acknowledgment of callsign)
classes.ads_b: Required (1090ES or UAT) within Class C and above the ceiling within lateral boundaries up to 10,000' MSL
classes.speed_limit: 200 KIAS at or below 2,500' AGL within 4 NM of primary airport

chart_depiction.shape: Two concentric rings (inner 5 NM surface-up, outer 10 NM shelf)
chart_depiction.description: Solid magenta lines with MSL altitude labels

classes.notes: Two rings: inner 5 NM surface-up, outer 10 NM shelf. '[Your callsign] standby' from ATC counts as established communication; a bare 'standby' without your callsign does NOT.

classes.entry_requirement: Two-way radio contact established with ATC
classes.transponder: Not required by class (required if within Mode C veil of overlying Class B)
classes.ads_b: Not required by class (required if within ADS-B rule airspace)
classes.speed_limit: 200 KIAS at or below 2,500' AGL within 4 NM of primary airport

chart_depiction.description: Dashed blue lines around the airport symbol

classes.notes: Reverts to Class E or G when tower is closed — check Chart Supplement for the specific airport to determine which.

classes.altitude: Varies — commonly 700' AGL, 1,200' AGL, or surface; up to but not including 18,000' MSL
classes.location: Controlled airspace not classified as A, B, C, or D
classes.transponder: Required above 10,000' MSL (excluding at/below 2,500' AGL) and within 30 NM of Class B primary airport (Mode C veil)
classes.ads_b: Required at and above 10,000' MSL in contiguous 48 states (excluding at/below 2,500' AGL); within Class B Mode C veil

chart_depiction.shape: Varies — shaded magenta vignette for 700' floors, dashed magenta for surface-based Class E
chart_depiction.description: Magenta vignette (fading shading) indicates 700' AGL floor; dashed magenta line indicates surface-based Class E; blue (cyan) vignette for explicitly charted 1,200' AGL floor; no depiction where 1,200' AGL is the default

classes.notes: Most common airspace in the US. IFR traffic separated by ATC; VFR traffic gets services only workload permitting.

classes.altitude: Surface up to the base of overlying Class E (typically 700' or 1,200' AGL)
classes.location: Uncontrolled airspace, mostly rural/remote areas at low altitude
classes.transponder: Not required (unless within Mode C veil or above 10,000' MSL)
classes.ads_b: Not required (unless within ADS-B rule airspace)
classes.speed_limit: 250 KIAS below 10,000' MSL (14 CFR 91.117 applies everywhere)

chart_depiction.description: Not directly depicted — Class G is the absence of depicted controlled airspace. Exists below the floor of Class E.

classes.notes: Uncontrolled — no ATC authority. Pilot is solely responsible for see-and-avoid. Most lenient weather minimums during daytime below 1,200' AGL.

glossary.MSL: Mean Sea Level — altitude measured from sea level datum
glossary.AGL: Above Ground Level — altitude measured from terrain surface
glossary.FL: Flight Level — pressure altitude in hundreds of feet (e.g., FL180 = 18,000')
glossary.Mode_C: Transponder mode that reports aircraft altitude to ATC via secondary radar
glossary.ADS_B_Out: Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast — GPS-based position/altitude broadcast, required since Jan 1 2020 in most transponder-required airspace
glossary.1090ES: 1090 MHz Extended Squitter — ADS-B transmit frequency, required in Class A, usable in all classes
glossary.UAT: Universal Access Transceiver — 978 MHz ADS-B option, usable below 18,000' MSL only
glossary.Mode_C_Veil: The 30 NM ring around a Class B primary airport where Mode C transponder and ADS-B Out are required regardless of airspace class (surface to 10,000' MSL)
glossary.SVFR: Special VFR — ATC authorization to operate VFR in controlled airspace below basic VFR weather minimums (visibility >= 1 SM, clear of clouds)
